evals.prompt:
What's the security issue with this code?

```swift
UserDefaults.standard.set(authToken, forKey: "userAuthToken")
```

assertions.text: identifies plaintext storage vulnerability
recommends Keychain as alternative
references MASVS-STORAGE-1

evals.name: storage-simple

assertions.text: flags accessToken storage as CRITICAL
correctly identifies theme/onboarding as NOT findings (non-sensitive)
provides Keychain fix with kSecAttrAccessible recommendation

evals.name: storage-medium

evals.prompt:
This is an L2 fintech app. Audit the Keychain usage:

```swift
func storeCredential(_ token: String) {
    let query: [String: Any] = [
        kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
        kSecAttrAccount as String: "userToken",
        kSecValueData as String: token.data(using: .utf8)!,
        kSecAttrAccessible as String: kSecAttrAccessibleAlways
    ]
    SecItemAdd(query as CFDictionary, nil)
}
```

assertions.text: flags kSecAttrAccessibleAlways as HIGH (deprecated since iOS 12)
provides kSecAttrAccessible hierarchy (most to least secure)
recommends kSecAttrAccessibleWhenPasscodeSetThisDeviceOnly for L2
references MASVS-STORAGE-1 and MASWE-0005

evals.name: storage-complex

evals.prompt:
What's the security issue?

```swift
let apiKey = "sk-proj-abc123def456"
```

assertions.text: identifies hardcoded secret as CRITICAL
mentions extractable from binary via strings command
recommends Keychain or server-side config

evals.name: crypto-simple

assertions.text: flags hardcoded key as CRITICAL (C4)
flags zero IV as CRITICAL (C6)
recommends Secure Enclave for key storage
recommends letting CryptoKit generate nonce automatically

evals.name: crypto-medium

assertions.text: flags password used directly as key (no KDF) as CRITICAL
flags ECB mode (kCCOptionECBMode) as HIGH - preserves plaintext patterns
flags NULL IV as CRITICAL
recommends PBKDF2 (CCKeyDerivationPBKDF) for password-based key derivation
references MASVS-CRYPTO-1 and/or MASVS-CRYPTO-2

evals.name: crypto-complex

evals.prompt:
Is this Info.plist configuration secure?

```xml
<key>NSAppTransportSecurity</key>
<dict>
    <key>NSAllowsArbitraryLoads</key>
    <true/>
</dict>
```

assertions.text: identifies globally disabled ATS as CRITICAL
mentions allows plaintext HTTP to any server
recommends NSExceptionDomains for justified exceptions only

evals.name: network-simple

assertions.text: flags missing certificate pinning as HIGH for L2 app
explains MITM attack risk with rogue CA
provides URLSessionDelegate implementation with SecTrustEvaluateWithError
references MASVS-NETWORK-2

evals.name: network-medium

evals.prompt:
This is an L1 general-purpose app. Audit the network security:

```swift
class APIClient {
    private let session = URLSession(configuration: .default)
    
    func fetchData(from url: URL) async throws -> Data {
        let (data, _) = try await session.data(from: url)
        return data
    }
}
```

Info.plist:
```xml
<key>NSAppTransportSecurity</key>
<dict>
    <key>NSExceptionDomains</key>
    <dict>
        <key>legacy-api.example.com</key>
        <dict>
            <key>NSExceptionAllowsInsecureHTTPLoads</key>
            <true/>
        </dict>
    </dict>
</dict>
```

assertions.text: missing pinning is INFORMATIONAL for L1 (not HIGH)
ATS exception for single domain is acceptable with justification
correctly distinguishes L1 vs L2 requirements
does NOT demand L2 controls (pinning required) for L1 app

evals.name: network-complex

evals.prompt:
What's the security concern with UIWebView?

```swift
let webView = UIWebView(frame: view.bounds)
webView.loadRequest(URLRequest(url: url))
```

assertions.text: flags UIWebView as HIGH - deprecated since iOS 12
mentions App Store rejection risk
recommends WKWebView as replacement

evals.name: platform-simple

assertions.text: flags unvalidated input from URL as HIGH
force unwrap on query parameter is crash risk
payment action via URL scheme needs authentication check
references MASVS-PLATFORM-1 or MASVS-CODE-4

evals.name: platform-medium

assertions.text: injecting auth token into JavaScript context is HIGH risk
forMainFrameOnly: false exposes token to iframes
javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically enables popup attacks
loadPage accepts arbitrary URL without allowlist validation
references MASVS-PLATFORM-2 (WebView security)

evals.name: platform-complex

assertions.text: identifies format string vulnerability
NSLog(variable) without format specifier is HIGH
recommends NSLog(@"%@", action) with format specifier

evals.name: objc-simple

assertions.text: flags unarchiveObjectWithData as CRITICAL (C5)
explains object substitution attack risk
recommends unarchivedObjectOfClass:fromData:error:
references MASVS-CODE-4 and/or MASWE-0058

evals.name: objc-medium

assertions.text: method swizzling in +load is detectable by attackers
validateLicense can be bypassed via runtime manipulation
recommends moving security logic to Swift (no ObjC runtime exposure)
references MASVS-RESILIENCE for anti-tampering
mentions Frida/Cycript can hook ObjC methods at runtime

evals.name: objc-complex

evals.prompt: What compliance standard requires encryption of health data at rest?

assertions.text: identifies HIPAA
mentions technical safeguards requirement
references encryption at rest (§164.312(a)(2)(iv))

evals.name: compliance-simple

assertions.text: PCI DSS applies to credit card data
flags UserDefaults even with encryption (backup exposure)
recommends Keychain with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly
mentions PCI DSS requirement 3.4 (render PAN unreadable)

evals.name: compliance-medium

assertions.text: missing certificate pinning violates HIPAA transmission security
.completeFileProtection is good but audit log is missing
HIPAA requires access controls and audit trails (§164.312)
missing biometric auth with server binding for L2
maps findings to specific HIPAA technical safeguards

evals.name: compliance-complex

evals.prompt:
Will this app be rejected from the App Store?

```swift
let webView = UIWebView()
```

assertions.text: yes, UIWebView causes App Store rejection since April 2020
recommends WKWebView replacement
check dependencies for UIWebView references too

evals.name: appstore-simple

evals.prompt:
Check this app for App Store rejection risks:

- Uses AdSupport framework
- No ATTrackingManager.requestTrackingAuthorization call
- No NSUserTrackingUsageDescription in Info.plist

assertions.text: ATT required when using IDFA (AdSupport)
missing NSUserTrackingUsageDescription causes rejection
must call requestTrackingAuthorization before accessing IDFA
references specific App Store Review Guideline (5.1.2)

evals.name: appstore-medium

evals.prompt:
Pre-release security gate: This app targets iOS 17+ and uses:
- Third-party analytics SDK (Firebase)
- Location services
- Push notifications
- In-app purchases

What App Store rejection risks exist and what privacy manifest requirements apply?

assertions.text: privacy manifest (PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy) required for iOS 17+
third-party SDKs must provide their own privacy manifests
NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes must declare API usage reasons
NSPrivacyTrackingDomains for tracking domains
location requires NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription or Always

evals.name: appstore-complex

evals.prompt: What severity level is a hardcoded API key?

assertions.text: CRITICAL severity
extractable from binary

evals.name: audit-process-simple

evals.prompt:
A security audit found MD5 used for file checksums:

```swift
let checksum = Insecure.MD5.hash(data: fileData)
```

What severity should this be flagged as?

assertions.text: NOT a finding or LOW/informational (not used for security)
MD5 for non-security checksums is acceptable
only flag when used for password hashing or signatures

evals.name: audit-process-medium

evals.prompt:
Generate a security audit finding for this code in proper format:

```swift
// AuthService.swift line 42
UserDefaults.standard.set(refreshToken, forKey: "refreshToken")
```

assertions.text: includes file path and line number
maps to MASVS-STORAGE-1
includes MASWE ID (MASWE-0005)
provides concrete fix code with Keychain
explains risk (what attacker can achieve)

evals.name: audit-process-complex
